deviations.description: The new precondition introduces a requirement to terminate the phone company process if it takes more than 30 days, but the BPMN model does not include any activity or event that terminates the process after 30 days. The process can continue beyond 30 days without termination.
The modality changes from permission to obligation, requiring the process to be terminated if it exceeds 30 days. The BPMN model does not enforce this obligation, resulting in a violation.

The compliance pattern changes from a time duration constraint on sending the SIM card to the existence of a termination action for the process. The BPMN model does not contain any activity named 'terminate phone company process' or equivalent, so the new requirement is not fulfilled.
The dimension changes from time to control flow, requiring a specific control flow action (termination) that is not present in the BPMN model.
The required activity changes from 'send_SIM_card' to 'terminate phone company process'. The BPMN model does not include any activity or event corresponding to 'terminate phone company process'.

The temporal constraint on minimum duration is removed from the norms and moved to the precondition, but the overall non-compliance is already captured by the lack of process termination after 30 days.
